mnemonics.title: Twisted Pair Categories
mnemonics.content: CATegories: C3 -> 10, C5 -> 100, C5E -> 1G, C6 -> 10G/1G, C7 -> 10G, C8 -> 25-40G (Remember: 3=10, 5=100, 5e=1G, 6=10G, 7=Faster, 8=Greatest)

mnemonics.title: Fiber Types
mnemonics.content: Single-mode = S (Slim core, Long distance), Multi-mode = M (Meaty core, Medium distance)

mnemonics.title: Coaxial Cable Types
mnemonics.content: Thinnet (10BASE2) = Thin & Small (185m max), Thicknet (10BASE5) = Thick & Tall (500m max)

acronyms.usage: Most common Ethernet cable, no EMI shielding, used in LANs
Twisted pair with metallic shielding for EMI protection
External interference affecting signal quality in copper cables
Interference measured at the sending end of the cable
Interference measured at the receiving end of the cable
Push-pull fiber optic connector commonly used in telecommunications
Bayonet-style fiber optic connector, common in LANs
Small form factor fiber optic connector, popular in data centers
Connector used with coaxial cables in older Ethernet networks

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two wires twisted like a DNA helix - the twists help cancel out EMI, similar to how noise-canceling headphones work
Think of fiber optic like a water pipe - light bounces inside at angles (multi-mode) or goes straight through (single-mode), just like water reflecting off pipe walls
Layer-by-layer: Center copper core (1) -> Insulation (2) -> Braided shield (3) -> Outer jacket (4) - like a cable's armor for signal protection
Picture signal as water flowing through a pipe - it gradually decreases like water pressure drops over distance
Imagine two telephone conversations next to each other - you can hear bits of one in the other, similar to signal bleeding between wire pairs

analogies.analogy: Guided media is like a train on tracks - the train (data) follows a fixed path (cable). Wireless is like a bird flying freely - no physical path required.

Single-mode is like a single-lane highway where cars go straight at high speed with no collisions. Multi-mode is like a multi-lane highway where cars can take different paths, potentially causing delays (modal dispersion).
Think of Cat5e as a 2-lane road (100 Mbps) and Cat6 as a 4-lane highway (1-10 Gbps) - both get you there, but Cat6 handles more traffic faster.
Coaxial cable is like a well-insulated wall - the center speaker (core) is surrounded by insulation and protected by a shield, keeping outside noise out like soundproofing.
A repeater is like a rest stop on a long drive - it gives the signal a 'rest' and refreshes it for the next leg of the journey.
